title: Unit 20 Learn Greek Grammar Masterclass: Perfect Tenses (I have Done)

script.text: Welcome... to the Learn Greek Grammar Masterclass for Unit 20: Perfect Tenses. In English... we say 'I have eaten'... 'I had seen'. This implies an action that is completed... but has a connection to the present. In Greek... we build this using a helper verb and a very special piece of the verb. I'm William... and here with me is Athina. Athina... what is the helper verb for the Perfect?
for the Past Perfect. But we don't just add a random form of the main verb. We add a frozen ending.
It’s very easy. You take the Simple Stem... the 'Snapshot' stem... and you add the ending
I have written. Only the helper verb changes. So if I want to say 'We have written'?
stays exactly the same. What about for passive or deponent verbs?
Then the ending is stressed at the end.
I have slept. I have been lost.
The pronouns must sit before the helper verb.
Το έχω διαβάσει. Της είχε μιλήσει.
I have read it. He had spoken to her.
Now... a native secret. Do Greeks use the Perfect tense as much as English speakers do?

Actually... no! In daily conversation... we usually just use the Aorist. Instead of 'I have eaten'... we just say 'I ate'. But if you want to emphasize that something is finished right NOW... use the Perfect.

This has been a very efficient lesson. You now have the tools to talk about finished life experiences and completed tasks. Practice with the 'Life Achievements' checklist. Excellent work... we'll see you in the next unit.
